properties.remark: OBSERVED PORTIONS OF POCATELLO AVE - THE MAIN STREET THROUGH AMERICAN FALLS SUBMERGED IN WATER THIS AFTERNOON.

5 TO 6 POWER POLES SNAPPED OFF JUST ABOVE THE GROUND BY OUTFLOW WINDS FROM A DECAYING THUNDERSTORM JUST SOUTH OF DARLINGTON NEAR THE MOUTH OF ANTELOPE CREEK. REPORTED BY THE PUBLIC WITH PHOTOGRAPHS TO KIFI LOCAL NEWS 8. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-0 TORNADO. THE DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 15 MINUTES WITH A PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 1.8 MILES. THE TORNADO FORMED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF S 700 W AND WILLIE RD. ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS WERE 80 TO 85 MPH.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 1 S MORELAND. TREE LIMB DAMAGE WITH ADDITIONAL DAMAGE TO GREEN HOUSE. RIPPED THE COVER OFF & TWISTED THE POLES AND CONNECTIONS. ONE SIDE FLATTENED. PEA SIZE HAIL ACCOMPANIED.
TREE LIMB DAMAGE WITH PEA SIZE HAIL.
MESONET STATION SAN SAND DUNES.

MULTIPLE PHOTOS OF A FUNNEL CLOUD AND TORNADO FROM PUBLIC SOURCES. A DAMAGE SURVEY WAS CONDUCTED, BUT NO CLEAR INDICATION OF DAMAGE WAS FOUND. THIS TORNADO OCCURRED IN AN AREA OF IDAHO DOMINATED BY OPEN RANGE LAND WITH SAGE AND JUNIPER. WHILE NO DAMAGE WAS FOUND, PHOTO EVIDENCE SUPPORTED CLASSIFYING THIS AS A WEAK TORNADO. TIME AND LOCATION ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
VIEWED FROM IDAHO FALLS
MESONET STATION ITD95 HWY 20/26.

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED ABOUT 30 DOWNED POWER LINES IN THE VICINITY OF W65N AND N115W ROADS IN THE KETTLE BUTTE AREA. NEARBY MESONET SITE LOCATED ROUGHLY 3 MILES WSW REPORTED A WIND GUST TO 57 MPH AT 455 PM MDT THURSDAY AFTERNOON. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR AND NEARBY MESONET OBSERVATIONS.
3 INCH TREE BRANCH DOWN. TIME APPROXIMATED.
SPLIT AND TOOK DOWN TALL PINE TREE, SLAMMING IT ONTO THE PATIO ROOF. PHOTOS ON SOCIAL MEDIA.
MESONET STATION SUG SUGAR CITY.
MULTIPLE TREES REPORTED DOWN ACROSS ST. ANTHONY. ONE TREE REPORTED AS ACROSS A POWERLINE AND ROCKY MOUNTAIN POWER HAS BEEN ADVISED. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

HAIL PICTURE SUBMITTED TO EAST IDAHO NEWS.COM WITH A FEW STONES 1 INCH OR SLIGHTLY LARGER IN DIAMETER. TIME ESTIMATED USING RADAR.
STREETS OF HAILEY IDAHO WERE COVERED BY SURFACE RUN-OFF FROM VERY HEAVY SHOWERS FROM A THUNDERSTORM
STREETS OF HAILEY IDAHO WERE COVERED BY SURFACE RUN-OFF FROM VERY HEAVY SHOWERS FROM A THUNDERSTORM.

PUBLIC REPORTS VERY HEAVY RAIN WITH REDUCED VISIBILITY AND FLOWING WATER ACROSS ROADS ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF HAILEY NEAR FRIEDMAN MEMORIAL AIRPORT. FURTHER NORTH IN THE DOWNTOWN AREA, HAILEY FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTS SOME STANDING WATER BUT ROADS ARE PASSABLE AT THIS TIME.
NICKEL- TO QUARTER-SIZED HAIL.
LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN ALONG E 400 N. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

LARGE SHED AND ADJACENT CONCRETE PAD THROWN ACROSS THE ROAD ALONG E 500 N IN THE AREA OF N 1800 E. IRRIGATION PIVOT OVERTURNED JUST SOUTH OF E 500 N AND JUST WEST OF N 1800 E. CROPS DEFOLIATED IN THE AREA. NWS STORM SURVEY ALSO FOUND A PORTION OF A SHEET METAL ROOF PEELED BACK AND DAMAGED ON A BARN. BASED ON LATE VISUAL SURVEY OF DAMAGE, CORRESPONDING RADAR DATA, AND INTERVIEW WITH RESIDENT, DAMAGE WAS LIKELY CAUSED BY STRAIGHT LINE THUNDERSTORM WINDS WITH NO CLEAR INDICATION OF A TORNADO. TIME OF DAMAGE ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

OVER A DOZEN TREES DOWN IN THE AREA OF N 1900 E JUST NORTH OF E 400 N, MANY SNAPPED OFF AT VARIOUS HEIGHTS. PORTION OF SHEET METAL ROOF AND ADJACENT PLYWOOD REMOVED FROM BUILDING AND FOUND IN DITCH. LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN ACROSS THE AREA. BASED ON LATE VISUAL SURVEY OF DAMAGE AND CORRESPONDING RADAR DATA, DAMAGE WAS LIKELY CAUSED BY STRAIGHT LINE THUNDERSTORM WINDS WITH NO CLEAR INDICATION OF A TORNADO. TIME OF DAMAGE ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
TREE FELL DOWN AND DAMAGED A HOUSE IN THE TOWN OF THORNTON. SHED ON TETON ROAD IN EASTERN MADISON COUNTY WAS DESTROYED.
SEVERAL LARGE LIMBS AND ONE LARGE HEALTHY TREE WAS SNAPPED NEAR LYMAN-UNION ROAD.
STORM CHASER ESTIMATED 60 MPH WIND GUST IN REXBURG.
LARGE TREE DOWN IN THE HEISE AREA. PHOTO PROVIDED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
TREE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED INCLUDING SEVERAL LARGE COTTONWOOD LIMBS. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WINDS CAUSED TREE BRANCHES TO FALL ONTO A BUILDING IN THE PONDS SUBDIVISION IN VICTOR. NO DAMAGE TO THE BUILDING WAS REPORTED. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST PARTIALLY BROKE 1 POWER POLE NEAR THE BASE ALONG N HOLMES AVE. POLE LEANING OVER THE ROAD. PHOTO SUBMITTED BY THE PUBLIC TO KIFI LOCAL NEWS 8 FACEBOOK PAGE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND INFORMATION FROM SOURCE.
MESONET STATION DW9069. STRONG OUTFLOW WINDS FROM SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS.

TOP HALF OF A PINE TREE SNAPPED OFF IN THE PARKING LOT OF NWS POCATELLO. TRUNK WAS APPROXIMATELY 10 INCHES IN DIAMETER AT THE POINT WHERE IT BROKE. PLASTIC RECYCLING ENCLOSURE ALSO DAMAGED ELSEWHERE ON THE PROPERTY. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON MEASURED WIND GUSTS.
POCATELLO REGIONAL AIRPORT. THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WINDS.
SMALL TO MEDIUM SIZE TREE BLOWN DOWN IN FRONT OF KPVI CHANNEL 6.
IDAHO STATE JOURNAL REPORTS THAT A LARGE ELECTRICAL ROAD SIGN FOR CONSTRUCTION WAS BLOWN ONTO I-15 AND SLAMMED INTO A PAIR OF VEHICLES.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL FOUND ON WEST PORTNEUF ROAD BY OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE. TIME OF OCCURRENCE ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

ABOUT 200 PEOPLE REPORTED WITHOUT POWER IN THE AREA OF HOLMES AND CLEVELAND. TIME BASED ON RADAR.
THREE BRANCHES DOWN FROM A COTTONWOOD TREE. LARGEST BRANCH WAS 4 INCH DIAMETER.
METAL ROOFING PEELED OFF OF THE YANKEE FORK RANGER BUILDING AND SIX TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG THE RIVER.
A 61 MPH WIND GUST WAS RECORDED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER IN CHALLIS AT 2 PM .

4 POWER POLES SNAPPED IN HALF IN THE ALLEY BETWEEN N APPLE ST AND N BIRCH ST. W 3RD STREET BLOCKED. LARGE TREE DOWN AT W 4TH ST AND N CHERRY ST. OTHER LARGE LIMBS DOWN ACROSS SHOSHONE. STORM SURVEY ESTIMATED STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 70-80 MPH. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND SURROUNDING REPORTS.

ROCK LAKE RAWS SITE.
LARGE TREE SNAPPED OFF ABOUT 7 FEET ABOVE THE GROUND IN BURLEY. REPORT AND PHOTO FROM THE PUBLIC VIA KMVT NEWS FACEBOOK PAGE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

LARGE TREE APPROXIMATELY 3 FEET IN DIAMETER WAS UPROOTED AND DOWNED INTO A RESIDENCE. SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. REPORT AND PHOTO FROM THE PUBLIC VIA KMVT NEWS FACEBOOK PAGE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
LARGE EVERGREEN TREE SNAPPED OFF NEAR THE BASE IN HEYBURN. REPORT AND PHOTO FROM SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
TREE BLOWN DOWN. REPORTED VIA KMVT FACEBOOK PAGE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
MESONET STATION MIN MINIDOKA.

LARGE SECTION OF TREE DOWN AND PARTIALLY BLOCKING 3RD ST JUST EAST OF H ST IN RUPERT. ALSO OTHER SMALLER TREE LIMBS DOWN ACROSS THE AREA. SHEET METAL DEBRIS NOTED STREWN AROUND THE CITY - SOURCE UNKNOWN. SURVEY ESTIMATES WINDS AT 60-65 MPH.

CONCENTRATED AREA OF DAMAGE. VERY LARGE TREE OVER 3 FEET IN DIAMETER WAS UPROOTED AND CRASHED INTO A RESIDENCE ON E 400 S JUST WEST OF S 350 E. ROOF AND BACK HALF OF THE RESIDENCE WERE DESTROYED. NO KNOWN INJURIES AT THIS TIME. SECOND LARGE TREE NEXT TO THE RESIDENCE ALSO UPROOTED. LARGE TREES SNAPPED OFF APPROXIMATELY HALF WAY UP ALONG E 400 S NEAR S 250 E. 2 OTHER LARGE TREES 2-3 FEET IN DIAMETER UPROOTED HALF WAY IN BETWEEN S 350 E AND S 250 E AND POWER POLE SNAPPED IN HALF. ALL DAMAGE WAS ORIENTED WEST TO EAST. POSSIBLE MICROBURST WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 75-85 MPH.
2 POWER POLES SNAPPED OFF NEAR THEIR BASES ON S POLE LINE RD JUST NORTH OF W 800 S. STORM SURVEY ESTIMATED STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 75-85 MPH ACROSS THIS AREA. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
2 POWER POLES SNAPPED OFF NEAR THEIR BASES ON S POLE LINE RD JUST NORTH OF W 800 S. STORM SURVEY ESTIMATED STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 75-85 MPH ACROSS THIS AREA. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
SEVERAL LARGE TREES DOWN AT RESIDENCE ALONG S POLE LINE RD BETWEEN W 900 S AND W 1000 S. STORM SURVEY ESTIMATED STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 75-85 MPH ACROSS THIS AREA. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
POWER LINES DOWN ALONG ID 27 BETWEEN 700 S AND 900 S. ALSO A PUBLIC REPORT OF MULTIPLE POWER POLES DOWN IN THE GENERAL AREA.

4 POWER POLES SNAPPED OFF NEAR THEIR BASES ALONG HIGHWAY 27 BETWEEN W 800 S AND W 900 S. LONG ROW OF IRRIGATION PIVOTS BLOWN OVER NEARBY. ALL DAMAGE INDICATIVE OF STRAIGHT LINE WINDS FROM THE WEST. STORM SURVEY ESTIMATED WINDS OF 75-85 MPH ACROSS THIS AREA. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
NEAR W 700 S AND S 300 W. PRIVATE WEATHER STATION.

LARGE ELECTRONIC CONSTRUCTION SIGN BLOWN OVER ALONG I-84 EASTBOUND JUST WEST OF THE I-86/I-84 INTERCHANGE. ORIENTATION MATCHES STRAIGHT-LINE THUNDERSTORM WINDS OUT OF THE WEST. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

TOP HALF OF LARGE PINE TREE SNAPPED OFF JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 24 NEAR ACEQUIA. NEARBY DECIDUOUS TREE WITH NUMEROUS BRANCHES BROKEN OFF NEAR THE TRUNK. STORM SURVEY ESTIMATED STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 70-80 MPH. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND SURROUNDING OBSERVED WIND GUST REPORTS.

AN OPEN ENDED TRAILER ROLLED ONE TURN TO ITS SIDE BY THE LIKELY MICROBURST, SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGING IT. ADDITIONALLY, A NEARBY HEAVY METAL PICNIC TABLE WAS FLIPPED AND SHINGLE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ON A MARINA FACILITIES BUILDING. A STORM SURVEY CONDUCTED ESTIMATES WIND GUSTS BETWEEN 70-80MPH. TIME CONFIRMED BY A WITNESS CAMPING AT THE RV PARK.

APPROXIMATELY 30 LARGE, HEALTHY HARDWOOD TREES EITHER UPROOTED OR SNAPPED DUE TO A LIKELY MICROBURST. ONE OF THE FALLEN TREES CAUSED MODERATE DAMAGE TO AN RV, WHILE ANOTHER DESTROYED A PICKUP TRUCK PARKED NEARBY. ANOTHER TREE FELL ONTO A BASEBALL FIELD FENCE. STORM SURVEY CONDUCTED ESTIMATES WIND GUSTS BETWEEN 70-80 MPH. TIME CONFIRMED BY A WITNESS CAMPING AT THE RV PARK.
